steps.title: Identify the contrast between problem and exception
steps.content: The first sentence establishes a general problem: sources are scarce for pre-nineteenth-century subjects. The second sentence shifts to Ron Chernow, who had access to 19,000 documents—an exceptional case. The transition must mark the shift from problem to exception.

steps.title: Apply the contrast signal
steps.content:
D marks the opposition, placing Chernow's abundant documents against the scarcity described.
  • A suggests Chernow exemplifies the problem
  • B agrees with scarcity despite Chernow's abundance
  • C illogically makes abundance follow from scarcity
. That matches choice D — D is correct.
